title: Replace raw degradation heuristics with causal latent tire-state features
summary: Recent F1 literature models tire degradation as a latent state rather than trusting raw observed degradation directly.
rationale: Recent F1 literature models tire degradation as a latent state rather than trusting raw observed degradation directly. A hidden wear/state estimate plus one-step innovation can capture whether the current lap is unusually bad for the true tire condition, which is more strategy-relevant than cumulative degradation alone.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Year,Race,Driver,Stint)`, fit a causal state-space or simple Kalman-style filter on lap-time delta and degradation using only past laps; export latent wear level, wear slope, residual surprise, and posterior uncertainty as features for the existing tree models.
expected_effect: Could improve generalization across tracks and compounds by denoising the most strategy-critical signal: tire condition versus expected pace decay.
risk: A badly specified filter can inject noise or hidden leakage if it accidentally uses future laps; implementation must be strictly forward-only within each stint.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
